with the VSTest@3 task, TRX reporting, and code coverage. We're on .NET 8 SDK.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a solution with 5 MSTest test projects all using VSTest (MSTest.TestFramework 3.8.0,
MSTest.TestAdapter 3.8.0, Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0). We use Azure DevOps
with the VSTest@3 task, TRX reporting, and code coverage. We're on .NET 8 SDK.
I want a complete migration plan to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.

arguments.file_text:
# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

## Problem
5 MSTest projects using VSTest runner (MSTest 3.8.0, Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0) on .NET 8 SDK with Azure DevOps VSTest@3 task, TRX reporting, and code coverage. Goal: migrate to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.

## Approach
MSTest 3.8.0 already ships with MTP support — no framework version bump required. The migration is primarily a **runner swap** (VSTest → MTP) plus CI pipeline changes.

---

## Phase 1 — Project File Changes (per test project)

### 1.1 Enable MTP runner
Add to each test `.csproj` (or a shared `Directory.Build.props`):

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
</PropertyGroup>
```

`EnableMSTestRunner` wires up the MTP entry point. `OutputType=Exe` is required because each test project becomes a self-contained executable.

### 1.2 Remove VSTest hosting package
Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` from every test project:

```shell
dotnet remove <TestProject>.csproj package Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk
```

This package provides the VSTest host — it conflicts with MTP and is no longer needed.

### 1.3 Add TRX report extension
MTP does not emit TRX by default. Add the extension:

```shell
dotnet add <TestProject>.csproj package Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport
```

### 1.4 Add code-coverage extension
For Microsoft code coverage (the built-in collector):

```shell
dotnet add <TestProject>.csproj package Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage
```

> If you use Coverlet instead, add `coverlet.collector` and pass `--collect "XPlat Code Coverage"` via `dotnet test`. Coverlet works with MTP through `dotnet test` passthrough.

### 1.5 (Optional) Centralize in Directory.Build.props
If all 5 projects share a `Directory.Build.props`, you can make all changes once:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <!-- Run tests in parallel across projects -->
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` from all test `.csproj` files.

---

## Phase 2 — Local Validation

### 2.1 Build
```shell
dotnet build
```
Fix any `OutputType` or entry-point conflicts.

### 2.2 Run via dotnet test
```shell
dotnet test --logger trx --results-directory TestResults
```
`dotnet test` still works with MTP — it detects the new runner automatically.

### 2.3 Run as standalone executable (optional verification)
```shell
./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyTests --report-trx
```
Each test project is now a self-contained executable.

### 2.4 Verify TRX files
Confirm `.trx` files appear in `TestResults/`.

### 2.5 Verify code coverage
```shell
dotnet test --collect "Code Coverage"
```
Confirm `.coverage` or `.cobertura.xml` files are produced.

---

## Phase 3 — Azure DevOps Pipeline Changes

### 3.1 Replace VSTest@3 with DotNetCoreCLI@2
The `VSTest@3` task uses the VSTest runner — it **cannot** invoke MTP executables. Replace it:

**Before:**
```yaml
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblies: '**/*Tests.dll'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true
```

**After:**
```yaml
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release
      --logger trx
      --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults
      --collect "Code Coverage"
```

### 3.2 Publish TRX results
```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Test Results'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.trx'
    mergeTestResults: true
```

### 3.3 Publish code coverage
```yaml
- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Code Coverage'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.cobertura.xml'
```

> If using Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage, generate Cobertura format:
> `--collect "Code Coverage;Format=Cobertura"`

---

## Phase 4 — Cleanup & Hardening

### 4.1 Remove any .runsettings VSTest-specific config
Review `.runsettings` files. MTP ignores most VSTest-specific settings. Migrate relevant settings (timeouts, parallelism) to MTP equivalents:
- Test timeout → `[Timeout]` attribute or `--timeout` CLI arg
- Parallel execution → `--parallel` or MSBuild property `<MaxFailedTests>`

### 4.2 Update any test filters
VSTest filter syntax (`--filter`) works the same under `dotnet test` with MTP.

### 4.3 Confirm no TestHost process conflicts
With MTP, each test project runs in its own process. If you had custom `TestHost` adapters or extensions for VSTest, they need MTP equivalents or removal.

### 4.4 Validate CI end-to-end
Run the full pipeline. Check:
- [ ] All 5 projects' tests pass
- [ ] TRX results appear in Azure DevOps Tests tab
- [ ] Code coverage report appears in Azure DevOps Coverage tab
- [ ] Test timing is comparable or better

---

## Key Differences to Be Aware Of

| Aspect | VSTest | MTP |
|--------|--------|-----|
| Runner | `vstest.console.exe` | Test project is the executable |
| Entry point | `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | `EnableMSTestRunner` + `Exe` |
| TRX output | Built-in | Requires `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` |
| Code coverage | Built-in via VSTest task | Requires explicit extension/collector |
| CI task | `VSTest@3` | `DotNetCoreCLI@2` (command: test) |
| Parallel | VSTest orchestrates | Each project = process; use `--parallel` |
| Startup time | Slower (reflection-based discovery) | Faster (source-generated) |

---

## Rollback
If issues arise, revert by:
1. Removing `EnableMSTestRunner` and `OutputType=Exe`
2. Re-adding `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`
3. Removing MTP extension packages
4. Restoring `VSTest@3` in pipeline

No framework code changes are needed — MSTest test code is identical on both runners.

---

## Todos

1. Update test project files (enable MTP, remove Test.Sdk, add extensions)
2. Centralize shared config in Directory.Build.props
3. Validate locally (build, run tests, check TRX + coverage output)
4. Update Azure DevOps pipeline YAML
5. Run full CI pipeline and verify results
6. Clean up obsolete .runsettings / VSTest config

data.reasoningText:
The user wants a migration plan from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform for their MSTest projects. Let me create a comprehensive plan for this migration.

Let me think about what this migration involves:

1. MSTest 3.8.0 already supports Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) - it was introduced in MSTest 3.4+
2. The migration involves switching from the VSTest runner to the new Microsoft.Testing.Platform runner
3. Key changes needed:
   - Update package references
   - Enable MTP in project files
   - Update CI pipeline (Azure DevOps) from VSTest@3 to `dotnet test` or direct executable
   - Handle TRX reporting with the new platform
   - Configure code coverage collection with the new runner, which may require adjustments to how coverage data is captured and reported

```yaml
  inputs:
    testAssemblies: '**/*Tests.dll'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true
```

**After:**
```yaml
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release
      --logger trx
      --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults
      --collect "Code Coverage"
```

### 3.2 Publish TRX results
```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Test Results'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.trx'
    mergeTestResults: true
```

### 3.3 Publish code coverage
```yaml
- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish Code Coverage'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.cobertura.xml'
```

> If using Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage, generate Cobertura format:
> `--collect "Code Coverage;Format=Cobertura"`

---

## Phase 4 — Cleanup & Hardening

### 4.1 Remove any .runsettings VSTest-specific config
Review `.runsettings` files. MTP ignores most VSTest-specific settings. Migrate relevant settings (timeouts, parallelism) to MTP equivalents:
- Test timeout → `[Timeout]` attribute or `--timeout` CLI arg
- Parallel execution → `--parallel` or MSBuild property `<MaxFailedTests>`

### 4.2 Update any test filters
VSTest filter syntax (`--filter`) works the same under `dotnet test` with MTP.

### 4.3 Confirm no TestHost process conflicts
With MTP, each test project runs in its own process. If you had custom `TestHost` adapters or extensions for VSTest, they need MTP equivalents or removal.

### 4.4 Validate CI end-to-end
Run the full pipeline. Check:
- [ ] All 5 projects' tests pass
- [ ] TRX results appear in Azure DevOps Tests tab
- [ ] Code coverage report appears in Azure DevOps Coverage tab
- [ ] Test timing is comparable or better

---

## Key Differences to Be Aware Of

| Aspect | VSTest | MTP |
|--------|--------|-----|
| Runner | `vstest.console.exe` | Test project is the executable |
| Entry point | `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | `EnableMSTestRunner` + `Exe` |
| TRX output | Built-in | Requires `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` |
| Code coverage | Built-in via VSTest task | Requires explicit extension/collector |
| CI task | `VSTest@3` | `DotNetCoreCLI@2` (command: test) |
| Parallel | VSTest orchestrates | Each project = process; use `--parallel` |
| Startup time | Slower (reflection-based discovery) | Faster (source-generated) |

---

## Rollback
If issues arise, revert by:
1. Removing `EnableMSTestRunner` and `OutputType=Exe`
2. Re-adding `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`
3. Removing MTP extension packages
4. Restoring `VSTest@3` in pipeline

No framework code changes are needed — MSTest test code is identical on both runners.

---

## Todos

1. Update test project files (enable MTP, remove Test.Sdk, add extensions)
2. Centralize shared config in Directory.Build.props
3. Validate locally (build, run tests, check TRX + coverage output)
4. Update Azure DevOps pipeline YAML
5. Run full CI pipeline and verify results
6. Clean up obsolete .runsettings / VSTest config

data.content:
No problem — here's the complete migration plan:

---

## Migration: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

MSTest 3.8.0 already ships MTP support — no framework version bump needed. This is a **runner swap**.

### Phase 1 — Project File Changes

**Per test `.csproj`** (or centralize in `Directory.Build.props`):

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Then for each project:
```
dotnet remove <Test>.csproj package Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk
dotnet add <Test>.csproj package Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport
dotnet add <Test>.csproj package Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage
```

`Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` is the VSTest host — remove it. The two extensions restore TRX and coverage, which MTP doesn't include by default.

### Phase 2 — Local Validation

```
dotnet build
dotnet test --logger trx --results-directory TestResults --collect "Code Coverage;Format=Cobertura"
```

Confirm `.trx` and `.cobertura.xml` files appear.

### Phase 3 — Azure DevOps Pipeline

**Replace `VSTest@3`** (incompatible with MTP) with:

```yaml
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: Run Tests
  inputs:
    command: test
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release
      --logger trx
      --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults
      --collect "Code Coverage;Format=Cobertura"

- task: PublishTestResults@2
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: VSTest
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.trx'
    mergeTestResults: true

- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.cobertura.xml'
```

### Phase 4 — Cleanup

- Review `.runsettings` — most VSTest-specific settings are ignored by MTP. Migrate timeouts/parallelism to `[Timeout]` attributes or `--timeout`/`--parallel` CLI args.
- Test filters (`--filter`) work identically.
- Remove any custom VSTest adapters/extensions — they need MTP equivalents.

### Key Differences

| | VSTest | MTP |
|---|---|---|
| Runner | `vstest.console.exe` | Test project is the exe |
| TRX/Coverage | Built-in | Explicit extension packages |
| CI task | `VSTest@3` | `DotNetCoreCLI@2` |
| Startup | Slower (reflection) | Faster (source-generated) |

### Rollback

Revert by removing `EnableMSTestRunner`/`OutputType=Exe`, re-adding `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, and restoring `VSTest@3`. No test code changes needed — MSTest source is identical on both runners.
